skill.name: git-protocol
skill.description: Git protocol implementation patterns using gitoxide for Guts repository operations
skill.summary: Git protocol implementation patterns using gitoxide for Guts repository operations
skill.author: AbdelStark

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown and Rust implementation examples, not executable skill scripts or malicious behavior. The Git HTTP receive-pack example is legitimate but should be implemented with authentication, input limits, and repository path validation.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to inline crate names and Rust fenced code blocks in documentation. No evidence found of shell execution, subprocess invocation, or install-time scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown list items or Rust code fences. The file contains no shell syntax or command execution API at these locations.

low_findings.title: Git HTTP Handler Examples Require Production Controls
low_findings.description: The network finding is a Git smart HTTP server example that parses request bodies and can update references. It includes a permission check, but implementers should add body limits, repository path validation, and packfile validation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code describes HTTP upload-pack and receive-pack handlers, so network-facing behavior is real in the example. It is not active code in the skill and does include an explicit permission check.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography finding points to the skill description. The only concrete cryptographic primitive shown is Ed25519 signature verification, which is not a weak algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata and does not specify a weak primitive. The implementation example later references Ed25519 verification.

low_findings.title: Repository Access Findings Are Git Object Operations
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings refer to repository object, commit, packfile, and reference operations. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited calls operate on Git repository data using gitoxide APIs. They do not inspect the host system or collect environment details.

content.value_statement: Implementing Git-compatible repository behavior requires correct object, packfile, ref, and smart HTTP patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rust examples using gitoxide.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to open or create bare repositories with gitoxide.
Outlines Git object reading and blob writing patterns.
Provides commit construction examples with signatures and parent commits.
Describes smart HTTP upload-pack and receive-pack handler structure.
Covers packfile generation and reference update patterns.
Includes a Guts-specific extended commit verification example.
limitations: Does not provide a complete compilable Rust crate.
Leaves several helper functions and error types undefined.
Does not include tests, benchmarks, or deployment configuration.
Requires production hardening for authentication, request limits, and repository path validation.

use_cases.title: Implement Git Storage Services
use_cases.description: Use the patterns to design repository object storage, commits, and reference updates in a Rust service.

use_cases.title: Prototype Smart HTTP Endpoints
use_cases.description: Map Git upload-pack, receive-pack, and info refs behavior to web handlers before building production code.

use_cases.title: Review Gitoxide Integration Plans
use_cases.description: Compare planned repository operations against documented gitoxide concepts and missing hardening work.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Gitoxide Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the git-protocol skill, explain the repository, object, commit, packfile, and reference flow in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Repository Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git-protocol skill to plan Rust repository operations for opening repositories, writing blobs, creating commits, and updating refs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Smart HTTP Handlers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git-protocol skill to design upload-pack and receive-pack handlers with authentication, validation, and request size controls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Gitoxide Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my gitoxide-based repository service design against the git-protocol skill. Identify missing protocol behavior, security controls, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: Plan a minimal Git-compatible repository service in Rust.
output_examples.output: A staged design covering repository creation, object storage, commit construction, smart HTTP routes, packfile generation, and atomic reference updates.

output_examples.input: Review my receive-pack design for safety gaps.
output: Apply refs atomically after packfile validation.

output_examples.input: Explain how Guts extends normal Git commits.
output_examples.output: A concise explanation of standard Git commit data plus signer identity, Ed25519 verification, and optional consensus round metadata.

best_practices: Treat all Git HTTP request bodies as untrusted input.
Use atomic reference updates when applying push commands.
Add tests for object parsing, packfile handling, permissions, and ref conflicts.
anti_patterns: Do not expose receive-pack without authentication and authorization.
Do not trust owner or repository path values without validation.
Do not apply packfiles before validating object integrity and size limits.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Git commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains Markdown guidance and Rust examples for using gitoxide APIs.

faq.question: Is the code ready to compile?
faq.answer: No. The examples are implementation patterns and omit some helper functions, imports, and error types.

faq.question: Which Git library does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on gitoxide, also known as gix, for Git operations in Rust.

faq.question: Can it help with Git smart HTTP?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines upload-pack, receive-pack, and info refs handler structure.

faq.question: Does it cover security controls?
faq.answer: It shows a permission check, but production systems need more validation, size limits, and authentication controls.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Rust developers, platform engineers, and reviewers building Git-compatible repository services can use it.
